```{r}
geo2.met.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(Dim.2 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, data = pca.df2) 
(g2mm2 <- Anova(geo2.met.mean.2, type = "III")) #all effects significant (all p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.met.mean.2)) #all good

    post_hoc2.2 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.2,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.2)
    pw2.2 <- pairs(post_hoc2.2)
    (pair2.2 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.2, adjust = "holm"))) #all Basins significant (p<0.001)


### Meristic Data
geo2.cnt.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(CatPC1 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, data = catpca.df2)
(g2cm1<- Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.1, type = "III")) # species (p<0.001) sig
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.cnt.mean.1)) #all good

    post_hoc2.4 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.1,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.4)
    pw2.4 <- pairs(post_hoc2.4)
    (pair2.4 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.4, adjust = "holm"))) #all Basins significant (p<0.001)

    
#default gaussian family still failed residual tests; switching to Student-t distribution due to extreme tail ends
geo2.cnt.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(CatPC2 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, family= t_family, data = catpca.df2)
(g2cm2<- Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.2, type = "III")) #all effect sig: species (p<0.001), basin (p=0.022) and species:basin (p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.cnt.mean.2)) #all good

    post_hoc2.5 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.2,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.5)
    pw2.5 <- pairs(post_hoc2.5)
    (pair2.5 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.5, adjust = "holm"))) #San Antonio-Guadalupe (p<0.001), Upper SWTX Coastal (p<0.001), Lower SWTX Coastal (p=0.002), Rio Tamesi (p=0.031)

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## VARIANCE TESTS (Extract dispersion)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
geo2.met.var.1 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.1, ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp", type = "response")
gmv1 <- pairs(geo2.met.var.1) 
(pair2.6 <- as.data.frame(rbind(gmv1, adjust = "holm"))) #San Antonio-Guadalupe (p<0.001), Upper SWTX Coastal (p<0.001) and Rio Tamesi (p=0.033) significant


geo2.met.var.2 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.2, ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp", type = "response")
gmv2 <- pairs(geo2.met.var.2) 
(pair2.7 <- as.data.frame(rbind(gmv2, adjust = "holm"))) #none significant
   
    

### Meristic Data
geo2.cnt.var.1 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.1, ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp", type = "response")
gcv1 <- pairs(geo2.cnt.var.1) 
(pair2.9 <- as.data.frame(rbind(gcv1, adjust = "holm"))) #none significant

geo2.cnt.var.2 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.2, ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp", type = "response")
gcv2 <- pairs(geo2.cnt.var.2) 
(pair2.10 <- as.data.frame(rbind(gcv2, adjust = "holm"))) #none significant


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## TABLE
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric data 

## ensure all results are as df
post_hoc2.1 <- as.data.frame(post_hoc2.1)
post_hoc2.2 <- as.data.frame(post_hoc2.2)
geo2.met.var.1 <- as.data.frame(geo2.met.var.1)
geo2.met.var.2 <- as.data.frame(geo2.met.var.2)


# Combine means and variances per dimension 
m1_all <- left_join(post_hoc2.1, geo2.met.var.1, by = c("Species", "Basin")) %>% 
  mutate(Dimension = "PC1", 
         Mean_Str = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", emmean, SE.x), 
         Var_Str = sprintf("%.2f", response))

m2_all <- left_join(post_hoc2.2, geo2.met.var.2, by = c("Species", "Basin")) %>% 
  mutate(Dimension = "PC2", 
         Mean_Str = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", emmean, SE.x), 
         Var_Str = sprintf("%.2f", response))


# Bind all metric dimensions together and pivot wide to separate species columns
metric_combined <- bind_rows(m1_all, m2_all) %>%
  select(Dimension, Basin, Species, Mean_Str, Var_Str) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Species,
    values_from = c(Mean_Str, Var_Str))

# Extract Pairwise Post-hoc P-values from 'pair' data frames
p_means_met <- bind_rows(
  mutate(pair2.1, Dimension = "PC1"),
  mutate(pair2.2, Dimension = "PC2")) %>% 
  select(Dimension, Basin, p.value_mean = p.value)

p_vars_met <- bind_rows(
  mutate(pair2.6, Dimension = "PC1"),
  mutate(pair2.7, Dimension = "PC2")) %>% 
  select(Dimension, Basin, p.value_var = p.value)

# build tibble
postPCA_geo2_result <- metric_combined %>%
  left_join(p_means_met, by = c("Dimension", "Basin")) %>%
  left_join(p_vars_met, by = c("Dimension", "Basin")) %>%
  mutate(
    Classifier = "Metric PCA",
    Mean_test = case_when(
      p.value_mean < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
      p.value_mean < 0.05  ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_mean), "*"),
      TRUE                 ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_mean))),
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p.value_var < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
      p.value_var < 0.05  ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_var), "*"),
      TRUE                ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_var)))) %>%
  rename(Amazon_mean = `Mean_Str_Amazon molly`, Sailfin_mean = `Mean_Str_Sailfin molly`,
         Amazon_var = `Var_Str_Amazon molly`, Sailfin_var = `Var_Str_Sailfin molly`)

### Meristic Data

## ensure all results are as df
post_hoc2.4 <- as.data.frame(post_hoc2.4)
post_hoc2.5 <- as.data.frame(post_hoc2.5)
geo2.cnt.var.1 <- as.data.frame(geo2.cnt.var.1)
geo2.cnt.var.2 <- as.data.frame(geo2.cnt.var.2)

# Combine means and variances per dimension 
c1_all <- left_join(post_hoc2.4, geo2.cnt.var.1, by = c("Species", "Basin")) %>% 
  mutate(Dimension = "CatPC1", 
         Mean_Str = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", emmean, SE.x), 
         Var_Str = sprintf("%.2f", response))

c2_all <- left_join(post_hoc2.5, geo2.cnt.var.2, by = c("Species", "Basin")) %>% 
  mutate(Dimension = "CatPC2", 
         Mean_Str = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", emmean, SE.x), 
         Var_Str = sprintf("%.2f", response))


# Bind all meristic dimensions together and pivot wide to separate species columns
meristic_combined <- bind_rows(c1_all, c2_all) %>%
  select(Dimension, Basin, Species, Mean_Str, Var_Str) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Species,
    values_from = c(Mean_Str, Var_Str))

# Extract Pairwise Post-hoc P-values from 'pair' data frames
p_means_met <- bind_rows(
  mutate(pair2.4, Dimension = "CatPC1"),
  mutate(pair2.5, Dimension = "CatPC2")) %>% 
  select(Dimension, Basin, p.value_mean = p.value)

p_vars_met <- bind_rows(
  mutate(pair2.9, Dimension = "CatPC1"),
  mutate(pair2.10, Dimension = "CatPC2")) %>% 
  select(Dimension, Basin, p.value_var = p.value)

# build tibble
postCPCA_geo2_result <- meristic_combined %>%
  left_join(p_means_met, by = c("Dimension", "Basin")) %>%
  left_join(p_vars_met, by = c("Dimension", "Basin")) %>%
  mutate(
    Classifier = "Meristic CatPCA",
    Mean_test = case_when(
      p.value_mean < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
      p.value_mean < 0.05  ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_mean), "*"),
      TRUE                 ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_mean))),
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p.value_var < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
      p.value_var < 0.05  ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_var), "*"),
      TRUE                ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p.value_var)))) %>%
  rename(Amazon_mean = `Mean_Str_Amazon molly`, Sailfin_mean = `Mean_Str_Sailfin molly`,
         Amazon_var = `Var_Str_Amazon molly`, Sailfin_var = `Var_Str_Sailfin molly`)

  
## Grab global stats

global_mean_chisq_met <- c(g2mm1["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], g2mm2["Species:Basin", "Chisq"])

global_mean_p_met     <- c(g2mm1["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], g2mm2["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

global_var_chisq_met <- c(Anova(geo2.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], Anova(geo2.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Chisq"])

global_var_p_met <- c(Anova(geo2.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], Anova(geo2.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

global_mean_chisq_cnt <- c(g2cm1["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], g2cm2["Species:Basin", "Chisq"])

global_mean_p_cnt     <- c(g2cm1["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], g2cm2["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

global_var_chisq_cnt <- c(Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Chisq"])

global_var_p_cnt <- c(Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

# Combine global stats and map to dimensions
global_lookup <- tibble(
  Dimension = c("PC1", "PC2", "CatPC1", "CatPC2"),
  g_m_chi   = c(global_mean_chisq_met, global_mean_chisq_cnt),
  g_m_p     = c(global_mean_p_met, global_mean_p_cnt),
  g_v_chi   = c(global_var_chisq_met, global_var_chisq_cnt),
  g_v_p     = c(global_var_p_met, global_var_p_cnt))


##Combine tables
geo2_results_combined <- bind_rows(postPCA_geo2_result, postCPCA_geo2_result) %>%
  left_join(global_lookup, by = "Dimension") %>%
  group_by(Classifier, Dimension) %>%
   mutate(
  gmp_adj = p.adjust(g_m_p, method = "holm"),
  gvp_adj = p.adjust(g_v_p, method = "holm")) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean_test = if_else(gmp_adj >= 0.05, "&mdash;", Mean_test),
    Variance_test = if_else(gvp_adj >= 0.05, "&mdash;", Variance_test),
    
    mean_str = case_when(
      gmp_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("Mean: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_m_chi), ", *p*<0.001*"),
      gmp_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("Mean: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_m_chi), ", *p*=", sprintf("%.3f", gmp_adj), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("Mean: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_m_chi), ", *p*=", sprintf("%.3f", gmp_adj))),
    
    var_str = case_when(
      gvp_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("Var: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_v_chi), ", *p*<0.001*"),
      gvp_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("Var: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_v_chi), ", *p*=", sprintf("%.3f", gvp_adj), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("Var: &chi;<sup>2</sup>=", sprintf("%.2f", g_v_chi), ", *p*=", sprintf("%.3f", gvp_adj))),
    
    Dimension_Display = case_when(
      row_number() == 1 ~ paste0("**", Dimension, "**"),
      row_number() == 2 ~ mean_str,
      row_number() == 3 ~ var_str,
      TRUE              ~ "")) %>%
  ungroup()

#build table 
postPCA_geo2_table <- geo2_results_combined %>%
  select(
    Classifier,
    Dimension_Display,
    Basin,
    Amazon_mean,
    Sailfin_mean,
    Mean_test,
    Amazon_var,
    Sailfin_var,
    Variance_test) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Classifier") %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  cols_label(
    Dimension_Display = " ",
    Basin             = " ",
    Amazon_mean       = md("**Amazon**"),
    Sailfin_mean      = md("**Sailfin**"),
    Mean_test         = md("Pairwise Mean Contrast<br>(adj. *p*)"), 
    Amazon_var        = md("**Amazon**"),
    Sailfin_var       = md("**Sailfin**"),
    Variance_test     = md("Pairwise Var Contrast<br>(adj. *p*)")) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = html("Est. Marginal Mean (&plusmn;SE)"),
    columns = c(Amazon_mean, Sailfin_mean)) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Est. Variance",
    columns = c(Amazon_var, Sailfin_var)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold") %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = c("top", "bottom"), color = "transparent"),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = Dimension_Display)) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(align = "left", size = "small"),
    locations = cells_body(
      columns = Dimension_Display,
      rows = grepl("Mean:|Var:", Dimension_Display))) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Dimension_Display, Basin, Sailfin_mean, Mean_test, Sailfin_var))) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "lightgray", weight = px(2.5)),
    locations = cells_body(rows = Basin == "Río Tamesí")) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = -Dimension_Display) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 1.5)

### Final table
postPCA_geo2_table





```
***
***

## **Tampico MX Analysis**

For this analysis, we will compare sailfin, amazon and atlantic mollies from the Tampico, Mexico region. This is the proposed origin of Amazon mollies, and the only region in which all three species coexist. 

After filtering for individuals where the county/state information was recorded as Tamaulipas/MX, we have 32 sailfin molly, 28 Amazon molly, and 32 Atlantic molly.

### Individual trait models

**CONCLUSIONS:** Body depth, predorsal length, and dorsal base length were best supported with an unequal variance model; in all cases, the sexual species (sailfin & atlantic) were more variable than the amazon except for predorsal length, in which amazons were more variable than sailfin (atlantic still more variable than amazon). All remaining metric traits were best supported with an equal variance model (aka no significant difference in variation among the species). About half of the meristic traits failed model comparisons due to lack of variation; fallback Levene's tests and models that did converge show no significant variation differences for any traits. *Slightly different from main analysis*


```{r}
#create separate df for metric and meristic (count) data
metric3 <- geo %>%
  select(-c(5:12)) %>%
  select(-c("FLA"))

count3 <- geo %>%
  select(-c(14:23)) #still includes SL for analysis



# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## METRIC ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Ensure Species is a factor
metric3$Species <- as.factor(metric3$Species)
metric3$Species <- relevel(metric3$Species, ref = "Amazon molly")

tam_results_list <- lapply(met.vars, function(trait) {
  
  # Build the formula string correctly
  form_str <- paste0("log(", trait, ") ~ log(SL) * Species")
  form <- as.formula(form_str)

  # 1. Homogeneous variance model (with safety catch)
  mod1 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = metric3, family = gaussian)
  }, error = function(e) { NULL }, warning = function(w) { NULL })
  
  mod2 <- tryCatch({
    withCallingHandlers({
      glmmTMB(form, data = metric3, family = gaussian, dispformula = ~Species)
    }, warning = function(w) {
      # If it's a bad warning, escalate to an error to abort the model gracefully
      if(grepl("NA/NaN|calc_loglambda|iteration|failure", w$message, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
        stop(w$message) 
      }
      # Otherwise, safely muffle minor warnings while the stack is still active
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning") 
    })
  }, error = function(e) { NULL })
  
  # Verify clean convergence of both models
  models_valid <- !is.null(mod1) && !is.null(mod2) && 
                  !is.na(AIC(mod1)) && !is.na(AIC(mod2)) && 
                  !any(is.nan(summary(mod2)$coefficients$disp[,2]))

  unique_species <- levels(as.factor(metric3$Species))
  
  # --- PATH A: MODEL CONVERGED CLEANLY ---
  if (models_valid) {
    # Compare models
    comp <- anova(mod1, mod2)
    pval <- comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
    delta_aic <- AIC(mod2) - AIC(mod1)
    
    # Run DHARMa residual check
    best_mod <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) mod1 else mod2
    best_mod_name <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) "Homogeneous (mod1)" else "Heterogeneous (mod2)"
    
    message(paste0("--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Trait: ", trait, " (Best Model: ", best_mod_name, ") ---"))
    sim_res <- simulateResiduals(best_mod, plot = FALSE)
    
    # Plot the residuals automatically
    plot(sim_res) 
    title(main = paste(trait, "-", best_mod_name), line = 2.5)
    
    # Extract predicted log-variance per species
    rg <- ref_grid(mod2, component = "disp")
    disp_means <- as.data.frame(emmeans(rg, specs = ~ Species))
    disp_means$Variance <- exp(disp_means$emmean)
    
    # Pivot to easily calculate the single global ratio
    ratio_df <- disp_means %>%
      select(Species, Variance) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio  = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio  = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = delta_aic, 
        LRT_p      = pval,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
      
  } else {
    # --- PATH B: FALLBACK TO SIZE-CORRECTED LEVENE'S TEST ---
    best_mod_name <- "Model Failed - Levene's Test Fallback"
    message(paste0("!!! glmmTMB Failed for Trait: ", trait, ". Running Levene's Fallback. !!!"))
    
    # Step 1: Strip out body size dependencies using linear model on log traits
    lm_check <- lm(as.formula(paste0("log(", trait, ") ~ log(SL)")), data = metric3)
    metric$Abs_Resid <- abs(residuals(lm_check))
    
    # Step 2: Run a formal Levene's-style test via anova on absolute deviations
    lev_test <- tryCatch({
      leveneTest(Abs_Resid ~ Species, data = metric3)
    }, error = function(e) { NULL })
    fallback_p <- if(!is.null(lev_test)) lev_test$`Pr(>F)`[1] else NA
    
    # Step 3: Calculate empirical size-corrected variances per group
    empirical_vars <- metric3 %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(Variance = var(log(!!sym(trait)), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
      complete(Species = unique_species, fill = list(Variance = NA))
    
    ratio_df <- empirical_vars %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio  = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio  = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = NA, # Marked NA so it shows as blank/dash in your table
        LRT_p      = fallback_p,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
  }
  
  # Append baseline trait tracker string
  ratio_df$Trait <- trait
  return(ratio_df)
})

tam_results_df <- do.call(rbind, tam_results_list)

tam_results_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(tam_results_df$LRT_p,
                             method = "holm")

tam.met.results <- tam_results_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.display = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0(sprintf("%.3f", p_adj), "*"),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_adj))) %>%
  mutate(
    Trait_Name = factor(
      trait.names[Trait],
      levels = unname(trait.names))) %>%
  arrange(Trait_Name) %>%
  relocate(Trait_Name, .before = everything())

tam.met.tbl <- tam.met.results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_hide(c(Trait, LRT_p, p_adj, "Sailfin molly", "Amazon molly", "Atlantic molly", Best_Model)) %>%
  cols_label(
    Trait_Name = "Trait",
    SA_Ratio = md("Sailfin / Amazon"),
    AA_Ratio = md("Atlantic / Amazon"),
    Delta_AIC = md("&Delta;AIC"),
    p.display = md("adj. *p*-value")) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Variance Ratio",
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio))%>%
  cols_move(
    columns = c(Delta_AIC, p.display),
    after = Trait_Name) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio, Delta_AIC),
    decimals = 2) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = p.display) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Trait_Name, Delta_AIC, SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio))) %>%
  cols_align("center", everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)
  
  
tam.met.tbl



# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MERISTIC (COUNT) ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
### NOTE: issues with convergence since variation is so small and data is truncated

tam_results_list_count <- lapply(cnt.vars, function(trait) {
  
  form_str <- paste0(trait, " ~ SL * Species")
  form <- as.formula(form_str)
  
  # Initialize status tracking flags
  mod2_failed <- FALSE
  levene_failed <- FALSE
  
  # 1. Fit Baseline Homogeneous Model
  mod1 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = count3, family = compois)
  }, error = function(e) { NULL }, warning = function(w) { NULL }) # Rejects if optimizer warns
  
  mod2 <- tryCatch({
    withCallingHandlers({
      glmmTMB(form, data = count3, family = compois, dispformula = ~Species)
    }, warning = function(w) {
      # Check internal optimization/evaluation warnings and escalate to fail cleanly
      if(grepl("NA/NaN|calc_loglambda|iteration|failure", w$message, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
        stop(w$message) 
      }
      # Muffle minor, harmless warnings safely
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning") 
    })
  }, error = function(e) { NULL })

  # Verify if both models successfully and cleanly converged
  models_valid <- !is.null(mod1) && !is.null(mod2) && 
                  !is.na(AIC(mod1)) && !is.na(AIC(mod2)) && 
                  !any(is.nan(summary(mod2)$coefficients$disp[,2]))

  unique_species <- levels(as.factor(count3$Species))
  
  # --- PATH A: GLMMTMB CONVERGED CLEANLY ---
  if (models_valid) {
    comp <- anova(mod1, mod2)
    pval <- comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
    delta_aic <- AIC(mod2) - AIC(mod1)
    
    best_mod <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) mod1 else mod2
    best_mod_name <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) "Homogeneous (mod1)" else "Heterogeneous (mod2)"
    
    # Run DHARMa diagnostics safely
    message(paste0("--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Trait: ", trait, " (Best Model: ", best_mod_name, ") ---"))
    sim_res <- simulateResiduals(best_mod, plot = FALSE)
    plot(sim_res) 
    title(main = paste(trait, "-", best_mod_name), line = 2.5)
    
    # Extract Model Variances
    mean_sl <- mean(count3$SL, na.rm = TRUE)
    pred_data <- data.frame(Species = unique_species, SL = mean_sl)
    pred_data$Mean <- predict(mod2, newdata = pred_data, type = "response")
    pred_data$Phi  <- exp(predict(mod2, newdata = pred_data, type = "disp"))
    pred_data$Variance <- pred_data$Mean * pred_data$Phi
    
    output_df <- pred_data %>%
      select(Species, Variance) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio   = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio   = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = delta_aic,
        LRT_p      = pval,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
      
  } else {
    # --- PATH B: CONVERGENCE FAILED -> FALLBACK TO LEVENE'S TEST ---
    best_mod_name <- "Model Failed Optimization - Used Levene's Test Fallback"
    message(paste0("!!! glmmTMB Failed for Trait: ", trait, ". Running Levene's Test Fallback. !!!"))
    
    # Run Levene's Test on the raw response variable across species
    levene_form <- as.formula(paste0(trait, " ~ Species"))
    lev_test <- tryCatch({
      leveneTest(levene_form, data = count3)
    }, error = function(e) { NULL })
    
    # Extract empirical variances directly from the raw data
    raw_vars <- count3 %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(Variance = var(!!sym(trait), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
      complete(Species = unique_species, fill = list(Variance = NA)) # handle missing classes
    
    # Calculate ratios using the empirical data
    wide_vars <- raw_vars %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance)
    
    # Pull p-value from Levene's test table
    l_p <- if(!is.null(lev_test)) lev_test$`Pr(>F)`[1] else NA
    
    output_df <- wide_vars %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio   = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio   = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = NA,   # AIC is invalid because the likelihood engine failed
        LRT_p      = l_p,  # Storing Levene's p-value here so it seamlessly passes into p_adj
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
  }
  
  # Append the raw trait identifier string
  output_df$Trait <- trait
  return(output_df)
})


# Combine into a clean data frame
tam_cnt_df <- do.call(rbind, tam_results_list_count)

tam_cnt_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(tam_cnt_df$LRT_p,
                             method = "holm")

tam.cnt.results <- tam_cnt_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.display = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0(sprintf("%.3f", p_adj), "*"),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_adj))) %>%
  mutate(
    Trait_Name = factor(
      trait.names[Trait],
      levels = unname(trait.names))) %>%
  arrange(Trait_Name) %>%
  relocate(Trait_Name, .before = everything())

tam.cnt.tbl <- tam.cnt.results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_hide(c(Trait, LRT_p, p_adj, "Sailfin molly", "Amazon molly", "Atlantic molly", Best_Model)) %>%
  cols_label(
    Trait_Name = "Trait",
    SA_Ratio = md("Sailfin / Amazon"),
    AA_Ratio = md("Atlantic / Amazon"),
    Delta_AIC = md("&Delta;AIC"),
    p.display = md("adj. *p*-value")) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Variance Ratio",
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio)) %>%
  cols_move(
    columns = c(Delta_AIC, p.display),
    after = Trait_Name) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio, Delta_AIC),
    decimals = 2) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = p.display) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Trait_Name, Delta_AIC, SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio))) %>%
  cols_align("center", everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)
  
  
tam.cnt.tbl

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## PLOTS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

met.long2 <- metric3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(SL, BD, CPD, CPL, PreDL, DbL, HL, HD, HW, SnL, OL),
    names_to = "Trait",
    values_to = "Value")


tam.met.plots <- ggplot(met.long2, aes(x=Species, y=Value, fill = Species)) + 
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(color= "#8D8680", size=2, alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y", axes = "margins") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10, base_family = "sans") + 
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"), 
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    strip.text    = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5",
                               "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00",
                               "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Sailfin molly" = "Sailfin\nmolly", 
                              "Amazon molly" = "Amazon\nmolly", 
                              "Atlantic molly" = "Atlantic\nmolly")) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Length or Depth (mm)")
tam.met.plots


cnt.long2 <- count3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(D, P1, A, P1.R, LLSC, SALL, SBLL, SBDF, FLA),
    names_to = "Trait",
    values_to = "Value")

tam.cnt.plots <- ggplot(cnt.long2, aes(x=Species, y=Value, fill = Species)) + 
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(color= "#8D8680", size=2, alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y", axes = "margins") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10, base_family = "sans") + 
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"), 
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    strip.text    = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5",
                               "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00",
                               "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Sailfin molly" = "Sailfin\nmolly", 
                              "Amazon molly" = "Amazon\nmolly", 
                              "Atlantic molly" = "Atlantic\nmolly")) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Count")
tam.cnt.plots


```

***
***

### Overall shape variation (PCA)

As before, we will assess overall shape variation among species using a ***principal component analysis (PCA)*** on centered and scaled data (all metric traits will also be log transformed to better approach normality). We will then perform a mean and variance test (e.g., ANOVA and Levene's test) to determine if overall shape variation differs between the species. 

As meristic traits are count data, a normal PCA cannot be applied. Instead, we will use a ***categorical principal component analysis (CATPCA)*** that uses optimal scaling to deal with discrete integers. 

#### *Loadings*

```{r}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## METRIC ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

#get size-corrected residuals
shape_residuals <- lapply(met.vars, function(trait) {
  # Fit a simple linear model of the raw trait against SL
  mod <- lm(metric3[[trait]] ~ metric3$SL)
  return(residuals(mod))
})

residual_df2 <- as.data.frame(shape_residuals)
colnames(residual_df2) <- met.vars

# run PCA
PCA.test2 <- prcomp(residual_df2, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

summary(PCA.test2)
(eig.val.test <- get_eigenvalue(PCA.test2))


# Since we corrected for size, we will focus on only the first 2 dimensions

ind.test <- get_pca_ind(PCA.test2)
head(ind.test$coord[,1:2])


pca.df2 <- cbind(metric3, ind.test$coord[,1:2])


# Will investigate the influence of each trait on the first 2 dimensions
(loadings <- PCA.test2$rotation[, 1:2])

sorted.loadings.1 <- loadings[order(loadings[, 1]), 1]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for PC1" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.1, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

sorted.loadings.2 <- loadings[order(loadings[, 2]), 2]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for PC2" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.2, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MERISTIC (COUNT) ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Remove SL from count df
count4 <- select(count3, -c("SL"))

# Run the Categorical PCA
# 'ndim = 2' extracts the first two principal components
# 'degrees = 1' treats the scaling transformations as piecewise linear (ideal for ordinal integers)

catpca.test2 <- princals(count4[, 5:13], ndim = 2, degrees = 1)

# Print the summary to see how much variance your new components explain
summary(catpca.test2)

(cat.loadings <- catpca.test2$loadings)

sorted.loadings.4 <- cat.loadings[order(cat.loadings[, 1]), 1]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for CATPC1" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.4, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

sorted.loadings.5 <- cat.loadings[order(cat.loadings[, 2]), 2]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for CATPC2" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.5, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

# Extract the eigenvalues (one per dimension)
eigenvals <- catpca.test2$evals

# Calculate the percentage of variation explained by each axis
# (Divided by 9 because you have 9 meristic traits)
pc1_percent <- round((eigenvals[1] / 9) * 100, 1)
pc2_percent <- round((eigenvals[2] / 9) * 100, 1)

# Quick print check to see your numbers (e.g., 24.5 and 14.2)
print(paste0("CatPC1: ", pc1_percent, "%"))
print(paste0("CatPC2: ", pc2_percent, "%"))

# 1. Extract the object scores for the first two dimensions
cat_pc_scores <- as.data.frame(catpca.test2$objectscores)
colnames(cat_pc_scores) <- c("CatPC1", "CatPC2")

# 2. Combine them back with your species and size metadata
catpca.df2 <- cbind(count4, cat_pc_scores)

```

#### *Plots*

```{r}

#### METRIC

tam.pca.1<- autoplot(PCA.test2, data = metric3, colour="Species", shape= 'Species', loadings=FALSE, loadings.label=FALSE, frame=TRUE, frame.type='norm', label= FALSE)+
  scale_color_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_shape_manual(name= 'Species', 
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = 17, "Amazon molly"=16, "Atlantic molly"= 3)) +
   guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA)))+
  ggtitle("PCA Plot of Morphology traits") + theme_classic() 
tam.pca.1



#### MERISTIC

tam.catpca <- ggplot(catpca.df2, aes(x = CatPC1, y = CatPC2, color = Species, fill = Species, shape = Species)) +
  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.15, type = "norm", level = 0.95) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Species",
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = "#78B7C5", "Amazon molly" = "#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly" = "#78B7C5", "Amazon molly" = "#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_shape_manual(name = "Species", 
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = 17, "Amazon molly" = 16, "Atlantic molly"= 3)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA))) +
  labs(
    x = "Categorical PC1",
    y = "Categorical PC2"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = paste0("Categorical PC1 (", pc1_percent, "%)"),
    y = paste0("Categorical PC2 (", pc2_percent, "%)")
  )

tam.catpca


```


#### *Post PCA tests*

**CONCLUSIONS:** PC1 and CatPC1 have significant mean differences but not PC2 or CatPC2. This is expected, as all three species have different body shapes and therefore occupy distinct morphospace. *Different from main analysis*

CatPC1 shows a significant difference in variation among the species, with sailfins exhibiting the highest amount of variation followed by atlantic; Amazons contain the lowest amount of variation. *Slightly different from main analysis*

Only sailfin and atlantic differ for CatPC1. *Slightly different from main analysis* This differs slightly from our individual trait results: in individual trait results, only a few metric traits showed support for a unequal-dispersion model, and none of our meristic traits showed support for unequal-disperson models. This indicates species differences in covariation among multiple  meristic features. 

```{r}
testmod <- lm(Dim.1 ~ Species, data = pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #levene's test sig

testmod <- lm(Dim.2 ~ Species, data = pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #levene's test sig

testmod <- lm(CatPC1 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #levene's test sig

testmod <- lm(CatPC2 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails KS test

#since some models fail residual checks, I will use a GLMM with a by-species dispersion factor and test mean and variance differences from that; default family=gaussian unless otherwise specified


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MEAN TESTS (GLMM w/ ~Species dispersion for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data

tam.met.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(Dim.1 ~ Species, dispformula = ~Species, data = pca.df2) 
(tmm1<- Anova(tam.met.mean.1, type = "III")) #significant (p=0.011)
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.met.mean.1)) #all good
    tph1 <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.1, ~Species)
    summary(tph1)
    tpw1 <- pairs(tph1)
    (tpair1 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tpw1, adjust = "holm"))) #sail/atl(p=0.022), amz/atl(p=0.022)
    
tam.met.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(Dim.2 ~ Species, dispformula = ~Species, data = pca.df2) 
(tmm2<- Anova(tam.met.mean.2, type = "III")) #significant (p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.met.mean.2)) #all good
    tph2 <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.2,  ~ Species)
    summary(tph2)
    tpw2 <- pairs(tph2)
    (tpair2 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tpw2, adjust = "holm"))) #all sig (p<0.001)
    
    

### Meristic Data


tam.cnt.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(CatPC1 ~ Species, dispformula = ~Species, data = catpca.df2) 
(tcm1<- Anova(tam.cnt.mean.1, type = "III")) #significant (p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.cnt.mean.1)) #all good
    tph4 <- emmeans(tam.cnt.mean.1,  ~ Species)
    summary(tph4)
    tpw4 <- pairs(tph4)
    (tpair4 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tpw4, adjust = "holm"))) #all sig (p<0.001)
    


#default gaussian family failed residual checks; changing to Student t-distribution due to extreme tails
tam.cnt.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(CatPC2 ~ Species, dispformula = ~Species, family = t_family, data = catpca.df2) 
(tcm2<- Anova(tam.cnt.mean.2, type = "III")) #not significant
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.cnt.mean.2)) #failed KS test but qqplot w/ good alignment
    tph5 <- emmeans(tam.cnt.mean.2,  ~ Species)
    summary(tph5)
    tpw5 <- pairs(tph5)
    (tpair5 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tpw5, adjust = "holm"))) #for table later
    

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## VARIANCE TESTS (Extract dispersion info for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
tam.met.var.1 <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.1, ~ Species, component = "disp", type = "response")
tmv1 <- pairs(tam.met.var.1)
(tpair6 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tmv1, adjust = "holm"))) #sail/amz (p<0.001, sail/atl (p=0.007)

tam.met.var.2 <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.2, ~ Species, component = "disp", type = "response")
tmv2 <- pairs(tam.met.var.2) 
(tpair7 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tmv2, adjust = "holm"))) #sail/amz (p=0.18), sail/atl (p=0.008)



### Meristic Data
tam.cnt.var.1 <- emmeans(tam.cnt.mean.1, ~ Species, component = "disp", type = "response")
tcv1 <- pairs(tam.cnt.var.1) 
(tpair9 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tcv1, adjust = "holm"))) #sail/atl (p=0.007)


tam.cnt.var.2 <- emmeans(tam.cnt.mean.2, ~ Species, component = "disp", type = "response")
tcv2 <- pairs(tam.cnt.var.2) #not significant
(tpair10 <- as.data.frame(rbind(tcv2, adjust = "holm"))) #for later table


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## TABLE
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric data 

## ensure all results are as df
tph1 <- as.data.frame(tph1)
tph2 <- as.data.frame(tph2)
tam.met.var.1 <- as.data.frame(tam.met.var.1)
tam.met.var.2 <- as.data.frame(tam.met.var.2)


## pull species-specific model results
# Means
amazon_m1  <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph1$emmean[1], tph1$SE[1])
atlantic_m1 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph1$emmean[2], tph1$SE[2])
sailfin_m1 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph1$emmean[3], tph1$SE[3])

amazon_m2  <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph2$emmean[1], tph2$SE[1])
atlantic_m2 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph2$emmean[2], tph2$SE[2])
sailfin_m2 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph2$emmean[3], tph2$SE[3])


Amazon_mean <- c(amazon_m1, amazon_m2)

Atlantic_mean <- c(atlantic_m1, atlantic_m2)

Sailfin_mean <- c(sailfin_m1, sailfin_m2)

# variance
Amazon_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.met.var.1$response[1], tam.met.var.2$response[1]))

Atlantic_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.met.var.1$response[2], tam.met.var.2$response[2]))

Sailfin_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.met.var.1$response[3], tam.met.var.2$response[3]))


## pull model stats
Chisq_mean <- c(tmm1["Species", "Chisq"], tmm2["Species", "Chisq"])

p_mean <- c(tmm1["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"], tmm2["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

#need to run an anova on dispersion for 3 species
Chisq_var <- c(Anova(tam.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"],
               Anova(tam.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"])

p_var <- c(Anova(tam.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
           Anova(tam.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])
  

## create tibble
tam_postPCA_result <- tibble(
  Classifier   = "Metric PCA",
  Dimension    = c("PC1", "PC2"),
  Amazon_mean  = Amazon_mean,
  Atlantic_mean  = Atlantic_mean,
  Sailfin_mean = Sailfin_mean,
  Chisq_mean   = Chisq_mean,
  p_mean       = p_mean,
  Amazon_var   = Amazon_var,
  Atlantic_var   = Atlantic_var,
  Sailfin_var  = Sailfin_var,
  Chisq_var    = Chisq_var,
  p_var        = p_var) 
  

### Meristic Data

## ensure all results are as df
tph4 <- as.data.frame(tph4)
tph5 <- as.data.frame(tph5)
tam.cnt.var.1 <- as.data.frame(tam.cnt.var.1)
tam.cnt.var.2 <- as.data.frame(tam.cnt.var.2)


## pull species-specific model results
# Means
amazon_c1  <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph4$emmean[1], tph4$SE[1])
atlantic_c1 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph4$emmean[2], tph4$SE[2])
sailfin_c1 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph4$emmean[3], tph4$SE[3])

amazon_c2  <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph5$emmean[1], tph5$SE[1])
atlantic_c2 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph5$emmean[2], tph5$SE[2])
sailfin_c2 <- sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", tph5$emmean[3], tph5$SE[3])


Amazon_mean <- c(amazon_c1, amazon_c2)

Atlantic_mean <- c(atlantic_c1, atlantic_c2)

Sailfin_mean <- c(sailfin_c1, sailfin_c2)

# variance
Amazon_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.cnt.var.1$response[1], tam.cnt.var.2$response[1]))

Atlantic_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.cnt.var.1$response[2], tam.cnt.var.2$response[2]))

Sailfin_var <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tam.cnt.var.1$response[3], tam.cnt.var.2$response[3]))


## pull model stats
Chisq_mean <- c(tcm1["Species", "Chisq"], tcm2["Species", "Chisq"])

p_mean <- c(tcm1["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"], tcm2["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

#need to run an anova on dispersion for 3 species
Chisq_var <- c(Anova(tam.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"],
               Anova(tam.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"])

p_var <- c(Anova(tam.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
           Anova(tam.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])
  

## create tibble
tam_postCPCA_result <- tibble(
  Classifier   = "Meristic CatPCA",
  Dimension    = c("CatPC1", "CatPC2"),
  Amazon_mean  = Amazon_mean,
  Atlantic_mean  = Atlantic_mean,
  Sailfin_mean = Sailfin_mean,
  Chisq_mean   = Chisq_mean,
  p_mean       = p_mean,
  Amazon_var   = Amazon_var,
  Atlantic_var   = Atlantic_var,
  Sailfin_var  = Sailfin_var,
  Chisq_var    = Chisq_var,
  p_var        = p_var) 
  


  
### Combine & form table

tam_results_combined <- bind_rows(tam_postPCA_result, tam_postCPCA_result) %>%
  group_by(Classifier) %>%
  mutate(
  p_mean_adj = p.adjust(p_mean, method = "holm"),
  p_var_adj = p.adjust(p_var, method = "holm")) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean_test = case_when(
      p_mean_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_mean), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_mean_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_mean), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_mean_adj), "*"),
      TRUE           ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_mean), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_mean_adj))),
    
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_var_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_var_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_var_adj), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_var_adj)))) %>%
  ungroup()




tam_postPCA_table <- tam_results_combined %>%
  select(
    Classifier,
    Dimension,
    Amazon_mean,
    Atlantic_mean,
    Sailfin_mean,
    Mean_test,
    Amazon_var,
    Atlantic_var,
    Sailfin_var,
    Variance_test) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Classifier") %>%
  fmt_markdown(
    columns = everything()) %>%
  cols_label(
    Dimension = " ",
    Amazon_mean = md("**Amazon**"),
    Atlantic_mean = md("**Atlantic**"),
    Sailfin_mean = md("**Sailfin**"),
    Mean_test = html("Mean comparison<br>(Wald &chi;<sup>2</sup>)"),
    Amazon_var = md("**Amazon**"),
    Atlantic_var = md("**Atlantic**"),
    Sailfin_var = md("**Sailfin**"),
    Variance_test = html("Variance comparison<br>(Wald &chi;<sup>2</sup>)")) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = html("Est. Marginal Mean (&plusmn;SE)"),
    columns = c(Amazon_mean, Atlantic_mean, Sailfin_mean)) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Est. Variance",
    columns = c(Amazon_var, Atlantic_var, Sailfin_var)) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold") %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Dimension, Sailfin_mean, Mean_test, Sailfin_var))) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 1.5)

### Final table
tam_postPCA_table

```

```{r}

#additional table of just the variances

### Metric data

#grab the anova variance test again
Chisq_var <- c(Anova(tam.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"],
               Anova(tam.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"])

p_var <- c(Anova(tam.met.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
           Anova(tam.met.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

#extract the ratios and p-values after adjusting
AA_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair6$ratio[1], tpair7$ratio[1]))
SAm_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair6$ratio[2], tpair7$ratio[2]))
SAt_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair6$ratio[3], tpair7$ratio[3]))

AA_p_raw <- c(tpair6$p.value[1], tpair7$p.value[1])
SAm_p_raw <- c(tpair6$p.value[2], tpair7$p.value[2])
SAt_p_raw <- c(tpair6$p.value[3], tpair7$p.value[3])

tam.pca.var.table <- tibble(
  Classifier   = "Metric PCA",
  Dimension    = c("PC1", "PC2"),
  Chisq_var    = Chisq_var,
  p_var        = p_var,
  SAm_ratio    = SAm_ratio,
  SAt_ratio    = SAt_ratio,
  AA_ratio     = AA_ratio,
  SAm_p        = SAm_p_raw,
  SAt_p        = SAt_p_raw,
  AA_p         = AA_p_raw) 


### Meristic data

#grab the anova variance test again
Chisq_var <- c(Anova(tam.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"],
               Anova(tam.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Chisq"])

p_var <- c(Anova(tam.cnt.mean.1, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"],
           Anova(tam.cnt.mean.2, component = "disp", type = "III")["Species", "Pr(>Chisq)"])

#extract the ratios and p-values after adjusting
AA_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair9$ratio[1], tpair10$ratio[1]))
SAm_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair9$ratio[2], tpair10$ratio[2]))
SAt_ratio <- sprintf("%.2f", c(tpair9$ratio[3], tpair10$ratio[3]))

AA_p_raw <- c(tpair9$p.value[1], tpair10$p.value[1])
SAm_p_raw <- c(tpair9$p.value[2], tpair10$p.value[2])
SAt_p_raw <- c(tpair9$p.value[3], tpair10$p.value[3])

tam.cpca.var.table <- tibble(
  Classifier   = "Meristic CatPCA",
  Dimension    = c("CatPC1", "CatPC2"),
  Chisq_var    = Chisq_var,
  p_var        = p_var,
  SAm_ratio    = SAm_ratio,
  SAt_ratio    = SAt_ratio,
  AA_ratio     = AA_ratio,
  SAm_p        = SAm_p_raw,
  SAt_p        = SAt_p_raw,
  AA_p         = AA_p_raw) 


### Combine & form table

var_results_combined_tam <- bind_rows(tam.pca.var.table, tam.cpca.var.table) %>%
group_by(Classifier) %>%
  mutate(
  SAm_p_adj = p.adjust(SAm_p, method = "holm"),
  SAt_p_adj = p.adjust(SAt_p, method = "holm"),
  AA_p_adj = p.adjust(AA_p, method = "holm"),
  p_var_adj = p.adjust(p_var, method = "holm")) %>%
  mutate(
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_var_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_var_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_var_adj), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chisq_var), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_var_adj))),
    
    Pairwise_tests = if_else(
      p_var_adj >= 0.05, 
      "&mdash;", 
      paste0(
        "Amazon/Sailfin: ", case_when(SAm_p_adj < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*", SAm_p_adj < 0.05 ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", SAm_p_adj), "*"), TRUE ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", SAm_p_adj))),
        "<br>",
        "Atlantic/Sailfin: ", case_when(SAt_p_adj < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*", SAt_p_adj < 0.05 ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", SAt_p_adj), "*"), TRUE ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", SAt_p_adj))),
        "<br>",
        "Amazon/Atlantic: ", case_when(AA_p_adj < 0.001  ~ "*p* < 0.001*", AA_p_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", AA_p_adj), "*"),  TRUE ~ paste0("*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", AA_p_adj)))))) %>%
  ungroup()




var_postPCA_table_tam <- var_results_combined_tam %>%
  select(
    Classifier,
    Dimension,
    SAm_ratio,
    SAt_ratio,
    AA_ratio,
    SAm_p, 
    SAt_p,
    AA_p,
    Variance_test,
    Pairwise_tests) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Classifier") %>%
  cols_hide(c(SAm_p, SAt_p, AA_p)) %>%
  fmt_markdown(
    columns = c(SAm_ratio, SAt_ratio, AA_ratio, Variance_test, Pairwise_tests)) %>%
  cols_label(
    Dimension = " ",
    SAm_ratio = "Amazon/Sailfin",
    SAt_ratio = "Atlantic/Sailfin",
    AA_ratio  = "Amazon/Atlantic",
    Pairwise_tests = md("Pairwise tests (adj. *p*)"),
    Variance_test = html("Variance comparison<br>(Wald &chi;<sup>2</sup>)")) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Variance Ratio",
    columns = c(SAm_ratio, SAt_ratio, AA_ratio)) %>%
  cols_move(
    columns = Variance_test,
    after = Dimension) %>%
  tab_options(row_group.font.weight = "bold") %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Dimension, Variance_test, AA_ratio))) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1), style = "dashed"), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(SAm_ratio, SAt_ratio))) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 1.5)

### Final table
var_postPCA_table_tam



```

***
***
